scenario: Jun 3: US strikes Qeshm Island (Al Jazeera); Iran drone hits Kuwait International Airport — 1 killed, flights suspended. Rubio rejects Hormuz-linked deal: sanctions only lifted on enriched uranium concession. Israel escalates Lebanon attacks despite Jun 1 Hezbollah halt. Brent $98.57 (TradingView Jun 3, +4.3% from Jun 2; third consecutive up). TTF €49.50 (TradingView, +11.2%). Tail 42% (up from 35% on Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport + Rubio rejection — triple escalation). Ships sailing dark through Hormuz.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat rises on Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport attack (Jun 3; Brent $98.57 +4.3%; third consecutive up). Tail 42% (up from 35%) — supply chain risk re-pricing on triple escalation day. Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting persist indefinitely. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Corn rises on crude correlation (Brent $98.57 Jun 3 +4.3%; US strikes Qeshm Island; Iran hits Kuwait airport). Biofuel demand outlook improves on higher oil. Cape routing disruption unchanged. Tail 42%.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Soybeans rise on crude correlation (Brent $98.57 Jun 3; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport attack). Bunker costs rebounding on escalation. Cape routing costs persist. Tail 42%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO rises on crude correlation (Brent $98.57 Jun 3 +4.3%; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport). Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia supply stable until July per Petronas assurance. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: Rice rises on food security repricing (Brent $98.57 Jun 3; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport — tail 42%). Qafco force majeure persists indefinitely; planting window pressure acute. ASEAN food security premium expands on Qeshm escalation.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha rises on crude (Brent $98.57 Jun 3 +4.3%; Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport). Gulf condensate interdiction persists until Hormuz physically reopens. Tail 42%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene tracks naphtha higher (Jun 3 est.; Brent $98.57 +4.3%; Qeshm strike). SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing. Tail 42%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol rises on crude (Brent $98.57 Jun 3; Qeshm strike). US Iran sanctions waiver expired; Qatar force majeure persists; OFAC Economic Fury enforcement unchanged. Tail 42%. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea jumps on Qeshm strike + Kuwait airport attack (Jun 3; Brent $98.57 +4.3%). Qafco force majeure lifting delayed indefinitely. Rubio rejects Hormuz deal — no near-term supply normalisation. Tail 42% (up from 35%). OFAC Economic Fury enforcement unchanged. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP rises on Qeshm escalation (Jun 3; Brent $98.57 +4.3%). Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption persists. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute. Tail 42%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight rises on oil (Brent $98.57 Jun 3; Qeshm strike). Cape rerouting persists. Palm oil plantations: input cost pressure rising. Tail 42%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI rises on Qeshm escalation (Jun 3; Brent $98.57 +4.3%). US strikes Qeshm Island extends P&I suspension and Cape rerouting indefinitely. Mine-clearing ~6 months from any deal — now further away. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates rise on Qeshm escalation (Jun 3; Brent $98.57 +4.3%). Qeshm strike targets IRGC naval base — physical reopening timeline extended. Mine-clearing ~6 months. Tail 42% supports elevated rate floor. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI rises on Qeshm escalation (pub pending; updated est. on Qeshm strike + oil spike). US strikes Qeshm Island — Cape rerouting premium extends indefinitely. Tail 42%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly, est. intra-week move).
